request.question: What is the population of the municipality which is located about 230 km Southwest of Dorgali?

title: Elvas
Dorgali
Tartarugalzinho
Abu Simbel temples

Holskardvatnet

sentences: Elvas (] ) is a Portuguese municipality, former episcopal city and frontier fortress of easternmost central Portugal, located in the district of Portalegre in Alentejo.
It is situated about 230 km east of Lisbon, and about 15 km west of the Spanish fortress of Badajoz, by the Madrid-Badajoz-Lisbon railway.
The municipality population as of 2011 was 23,078, in an area of 631.29 km2 .
The city itself had a population of 16,640 as of 2011 .

Dorgali (Sardinian: "Durgali" ) is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Nuoro in the Italian region Sardinia, located about 230 km northeast of Cagliari and about 38 km east of Nuoro in the Seaside Supramonte mountain area.
Tartarugalzinho ("Big Tartarugal") is a municipality located in the east of the state of Amapá in Brazil.
Its population is 12,563 and its area is 6,712 km2 .
Tartarugalzinho is located 230 km from the state capitol of Macapá.
It was established as a municipality on December 17, 1987.
The Abu Simbel temples are two massive rock temples at Abu Simbel (أبو سمبل in Arabic), a village in Nubia, southern Egypt, near the border with Sudan.
They are situated on the western bank of Lake Nasser, about 230 km southwest of Aswan (about 300 km by road).
The complex is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site known as the "Nubian Monuments", which run from Abu Simbel downriver to Philae (near Aswan).

The twin temples were originally carved out of the mountainside during the 19th dynasty reign of the Pharaoh Ramesses II in the 13th century BC, as a lasting monument to himself and his queen Nefertari, to commemorate his victory at the Battle of Kadesh.
Their huge external rock relief figures have become iconic.
Galliavola is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Pavia in the Italian region Lombardy, located about 50 km southwest of Milan and about 30 km southwest of Pavia.
As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 230 and an area of 8.5 km².

Verkhneuralsk (Russian: Верхнеура́льск ) is a town and the administrative center of Verkhneuralsky District in Chelyabinsk Oblast, Russia, located in the upper streams of the Ural River, 230 km southwest of Chelyabinsk, the administrative center of the oblast.
Population:  (2010 Census) ;  (2002 Census) ;  (1989 Census)
Holskardvatnet is a lake in the municipality Vik in Sogn og Fjordane county, Norway.
It is located just less than 100 m north of the municipality (and county) border with Modalen in Hordaland county.

The lake is 20 km south of the village of Ortnevik in neighboring Høyanger Municipality, about 13 km southwest of the village of Arnafjord, and about 28 km southwest of the municipal center of Vikøyri.
The 4.81 km2 lake is located at an elevation of 845 m above sea level.
Cagliari ( , or ; ] ; Sardinian: "Casteddu" ; ) is an Italian municipality and the capital of the island of Sardinia, an autonomous region of Italy.
Cagliari's Sardinian name "Casteddu" literally means "castle".
It has about 150,000 inhabitants, while its metropolitan city (including Cagliari and 16 other nearby municipalities) has more than 431,000 inhabitants.
According to Eurostat, the population of the Functional urban area, the commuting zone of Cagliari, rises to 476,974.
The Bransfield Basin is a back-arc rift basin located off the Northern tip of the Antarctic Peninsula.
The basin lies within a Northeast and Southwest trending strait that separates the peninsula from the nearby South Shetland Islands to the Northwest.
The basin extends for more than 500 km from Smith Island (South Shetland Islands) to a portion of the Hero Fracture Zone.
The basin can be subdivided into three basins: Western, Central, and Eastern.

The Western basin is 130 km long by 70 km wide with a depth of 1.3 km , the Central basin is 230 km long by 60 km wide with a depth of 1.9 km , and the Eastern basin is 150 km long by 40 km wide with a depth of over 2.7 km .
The three basins are separated by the Deception Island and Bridgeman Island.
The moho depth in the region has been seismically interpreted to be roughly 34 km deep.
Bikin (Russian: Бики́н ) is a town in Khabarovsk Krai, Russia, located on the Bikin River (a tributary of the Ussuri) 230 km southwest of Khabarovsk.
Population:  (2010 Census) ;  (2002 Census) ;  (1989 Census) ; 19,000 (1967).

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.summary_1:
Primary topic: Dorgali (Sardinian municipality) and bridge candidate: Cagliari (city/municipality).

- From passage: Dorgali is a "comune" in the Province of Nuoro, Sardinia.
- From passage (verbatim): Dorgali is "located about 230 km northeast of Cagliari."
- Missing fact / bridge: The population of Cagliari (the municipality located ~230 km southwest of Dorgali) is NOT given in the passages — population is the required missing fact.
- Ambiguity: target could refer to the city/municipality "Cagliari" or to higher-level entities named Cagliari (e.g., Province of Cagliari / Metropolitan City of Cagliari); treat "Cagliari (comune/city)" as primary candidate.

Next-hop retrieval (priority order):
1) Fetch "Cagliari" (Wikipedia) — inspect lede/infobox and "Demographics" or "Population" section for the municipality's population (include year).
2) If needed, fetch "Cagliari (comune)" / "Comune di Cagliari" (redirect) to confirm city-level population.
3) If ambiguous, fetch "Metropolitan City of Cagliari" and "Province of Cagliari" pages (lede/infobox) to distinguish city vs province populations.
hop2_docs: Cagliari | Cagliari ( , or ; ] ; Sardinian: "Casteddu" ; ) is an Italian municipality and the capital of the island of Sardinia, an autonomous region of Italy.  Cagliari's Sardinian name "Casteddu" literally means "castle".  It has about 150,000 inhabitants, while its metropolitan city (including Cagliari and 16 other nearby municipalities) has more than 431,000 inhabitants.  According to Eurostat, the population of the Functional urban area, the commuting zone of Cagliari, rises to 476,974.

Pula, Sardinia | Pula (Latin: Nora) is a "comune" (municipality) in the Metropolitan City of Cagliari in the Italian region of Sardinia, located about 25 km southwest of Cagliari.  As of November 30, 2015, it had a population of 7.422, and an area of 138.7 km2 .

Settimo San Pietro | Settimo San Pietro, Sètimu in Sardinian language, is a "comune" (municipality) of the Metropolitan City of Cagliari in the Italian region Sardinia, located about 9 km northeast of Cagliari, it had a population of 6,532 (2011 census) and an area of 23.2 km2 .

Maracalagonis | Maracalagonis, Mara-Calagonis in sardinian language (mara = marsh), is a "comune" (municipality) in the Metropolitan City of Cagliari in the Italian region Sardinia, located about 12 km northeast of Cagliari.  As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 6,961 and an area of 101.5 km2 .

Monastir, Sardinia | Monastir (Sardinian: "Muristenis" ) is a "comune" (municipality) of the Cagliari metropolitan area in the Province of South Sardinia in the Italian region of Sardinia, located about 20 km northwest of Cagliari.  As of 2011 census, it had a population of 4,505 inhabitants and an area of 31.8 km2 .

Metropolitan City of Cagliari | The Metropolitan City of Cagliari (Italian: "Città metropolitana di Cagliari" ) is a metropolitan city in Sardinia, Italy.  Its capital is the city of Cagliari and includes 17 comuni.  It was established by law in 2016 and replaced the Province of Cagliari.  The current president is the mayor of Cagliari, Massimo Zedda.  The resident population is approximately 432,000.  This figure can rise due to commuting into the functional urban area to approximately 477,000
